content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes an integer as input and determines whether it is an even or odd number. Print "Even" if the number is even and "Odd" if it is odd.
constraints: The input integer is within the range of -1000000 to 1000000.
The input is a single integer, not a collection of numbers or any other data type.
content.inputFormat: A single integer is given as input.
content.outputFormat: Print 'Even' if the number is even, 'Odd' if it is odd, followed by a newline.

solution.approach: To determine whether a number is even or odd, we use the modulus operator (%). If the remainder of the division of the number by 2 is 0, the number is even; otherwise, it is odd.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because the operation (checking if a number is even or odd) is constant time, regardless of the input size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because we only use a constant amount of space to store the input number and the result.
